1.1: Paul (humble), an apostle (sent one) of Christ (the Anointed One) Jesus (YHWH saves) by the will of God {Theou}, and Timothy (honoring God) our brother,
1.2: To the saints (holy ones) and faithful brothers (and sisters) in Christ (the Anointed One) at Colossae: Grace and peace to you? from God {Theou} our Father [God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ].
1.3: We always thank God {The?}, the Father of our Lord {Kyriou} Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One), when we pray for you?,
1.4: because we have heard about your? faith in Christ (the Anointed One) Jesus (YHWH saves) and your? love {agap?n} for all the saints (holy ones)—
1.5: the faith and love proceeding from the hope stored up for you? in heaven, of which you? have already heard in the word of truth, the gospel (good news)
1.6: that has come to you?. All over the world this gospel is bearing fruit and growing, just as it has been doing among you? since the day you? heard it and truly understood the grace of God {Theou}.
1.7: You? learned it from Epaphras, our beloved {agap?tou} fellow servant {syndoulou}, who is a faithful minister {diakonos} of Christ (the Anointed One) on our [your] behalf,
1.8: and who also informed us of your? love {agap?n} in the Spirit {Pneumati}.
1.9: For this reason, since the day we heard about you, we have not stopped praying for you? and asking God to fill you? with the knowledge (discernment) of His will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
1.10: so that you may walk in a manner worthy of the Lord {Kyriou} and may please Him in every way: bearing fruit in every good work, growing in the knowledge (discernment) of God {Theou},
1.11: being strengthened with all power {dynamei} according to His glorious might so that you may have full endurance (perseverance) and patience, and joyfully
1.12: giving thanks to the Father, who has qualified you? [us] to share in the inheritance of the saints (holy ones) in the light.
1.13: He has rescued us from the dominion of darkness and brought us into the kingdom of His beloved Son,
1.14: in whom we have redemption [redemption through His blood,], the forgiveness of sins.
1.15: The Son is the image of the invisible God {Theou}, the firstborn over all creation.
1.16: For in Him all things were created, things in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities. All things were created through Him and for Him.
1.17: He is before all things, and in Him all things hold together.
1.18: And He is the head of the body, the church {ekkl?sias}; He is the beginning and firstborn from among the dead, so that in all things He may have preeminence.
1.19: For God was pleased to have all His fullness dwell in Him,
1.20: and through Him to reconcile to Himself all things, whether things on earth or things in heaven, by making peace through the blood of His cross.
1.21: Once you? were alienated from God and were hostile in your minds, engaging in evil deeds (acts).
1.22: But now He has reconciled you by Christ’s physical body through death to present you? holy, unblemished (blameless), and blameless in His presence—
1.23: if indeed you? continue in your faith, established and firm, not moved from the hope of the gospel (good news) you? heard, which has been proclaimed to every creature (in all creation) under heaven, and of which I, Paul (humble), have become a servant {diakonos}.
1.24: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for you?, and I fill up in my flesh what is lacking in regard to Christ’s afflictions for the sake of His body, which is the church {ekkl?sia}.
1.25: I became its servant {diakonos} by the commission God {Theou} gave me to fully proclaim to you? the word of God,
1.26: the mystery that was hidden for ages and generations but is now revealed (made clear) to His saints (holy ones).
1.27: To them God {Theos} has chosen to make known among the Gentiles {ethnesin} the glorious riches of this mystery, which is Christ (the Anointed One) in you?, the hope of glory {dox?s}.
1.28: We proclaim Him, admonishing and teaching {didaskontes} everyone with all wisdom, so that we may present everyone perfect (fully mature) in Christ (the Anointed One).
1.29: To this end I also labor, striving with all His energy working powerfully within me.

2.1: For I want you? to know how much I am struggling for you? and for those at Laodicea, and for all who have not met me face to face,
2.2: that they may be encouraged in heart, knit together in love {agap?}, and filled with the full riches of complete understanding, so that they may know the mystery of God {Theou}, namely Christ \the mystery of God: Christ/,
2.3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
2.4: I say this so that no one will deceive you? by smooth rhetoric.
2.5: For although I am absent from you? in body (flesh), I am present with you? in spirit {pneumati}, and I delight to see your? orderly condition and firm faith in Christ (the Anointed One).
2.6: Therefore, just as you? have received Christ (the Anointed One) Jesus (YHWH saves) as Lord {Kyrion}, continue to walk in Him,
2.7: rooted and built up in Him, established in the faith as you? were taught, and overflowing with thankfulness.
2.8: See to it that no one takes you? captive through philosophy and empty deception, which are based on human tradition and the spiritual forces of the world rather than on Christ (the Anointed One).
2.9: For in Christ all the fullness of the Deity {Theot?tos} dwells in bodily form.
2.10: And you? have been made complete in Christ, who is the head over every ruler and authority.
2.11: In Him you? were also circumcised, in the putting off of your sinful nature, with the circumcision performed by Christ \in the cutting away of the body of the flesh, by the circumcision of Christ/ and not by human hands.
2.12: And having been buried with Him in baptism (submersion), you? were raised with Him through your faith in the power of God {Theou}, who raised Him from the dead.
2.13: When you? were dead in your trespasses and in the uncircumcision of your? sinful nature, God made you alive with Christ. He forgave us all our trespasses,
2.14: having canceled the debt ascribed to us in the decrees that stood against us. He took it away, nailing it to the cross!
2.15: And having disarmed the powers and authorities, He made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the cross.
2.16: Therefore let no one judge you? by what you eat or drink, or with regard to a feast, a New Moon, or a Sabbath.
2.17: These are a shadow of the things to come, but the body that casts it belongs to Christ \but the body is of the Christ/.
2.18: Do not let anyone who delights in false humility and the worship of angels (messengers) disqualify you? with speculation about what he has seen. Such a person is puffed up without basis by his unspiritual mind.
2.19: He has lost connection to the head, from whom the whole body, supported and knit together by its joints and ligaments, grows as God {Theou} causes it to grow.
2.20: If you? have died with Christ (the Anointed One) to the spiritual forces of the world, why, as though you still belonged to the world, do you? submit to its regulations:
2.21: “Do not handle, do not taste, do not touch!
2.22: These will all perish with use, because they are based on human commands and teachings {didaskalias}.
2.23: Such restrictions indeed have an appearance of wisdom, with their self-prescribed worship, their false humility, and their harsh treatment of the body; but they are of no value against the indulgence of the flesh.

3.1: Therefore, since you? have been raised with Christ (the Anointed One), strive for the things above, where Christ is seated at the right hand of God {Theou}.
3.2: Set your minds on things above, not on earthly things.
3.3: For you? died, and your? life is now hidden with Christ (the Anointed One) in God {The?}.
3.4: When Christ (the Anointed One), who is your? [our] life, appears, then you? also will appear with Him in glory {dox?}.
3.5: Put to death, therefore, the components of your earthly nature: sexual immorality {porneian}, impurity (uncleanness), lust, evil desires, and greed, which is idolatry.
3.6: Because of these, the wrath of God {Theou} is coming on the sons of disobedience.
3.7: When you? lived among them, you? also used to walk in these ways.
3.8: But now you? must put aside all such things as these: anger, rage, malice, slander (blaspheme), and filthy language from your? lips.
3.9: Do not lie to one another, since you have taken off the old self with its practices,
3.10: and have put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge (discernment) in the image of its Creator.
3.11: Here there is no Greek or Jew, circumcised or uncircumcised, barbarian (foreigner), Scythian, slave {doulos}, or free, but Christ is all and is in all.
3.12: Therefore, as the elect of God {Theou}, holy and beloved {?gap?menoi}, clothe yourselves with hearts of compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience.
3.13: Bear with one another and forgive any complaint you? may have against someone else. Forgive as the Lord {Kyrios} forgave you?.
3.14: And over all these virtues put on love {agap?n}, which is the bond of perfect unity.
3.15: Let the peace of Christ (the Anointed One) rule in your? hearts, for to this you? were called as members of one body. And be thankful.
3.16: Let the word of Christ (the Anointed One) richly dwell within you? as you teach {didaskontes} and admonish one another with all wisdom, and as you sing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs with gratitude in your? hearts to God {The?}.
3.17: And whatever you? do, in word or deed, do it all in the name of the Lord {Kyriou} Jesus (YHWH saves), giving thanks to God {The?} the Father through Him.
3.18: Wives, submit to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord {Kyri?}.
3.19: Husbands, love {agapate} your wives and do not be harsh with them.
3.20: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is pleasing to the Lord {Kyri?}.
3.21: Fathers {pateres}, do not provoke your? children, so they will not become discouraged.
3.22: Slaves {douloi}, obey your earthly masters {kyriois} in everything, not only to please them while they are watching, but with sincerity of heart and fear of the Lord {Kyrion}.
3.23: Whatever you? do, work at it with your whole being, as for the Lord {Kyri?} and not for men,
3.24: because you know that you? will receive an inheritance from the Lord {Kyriou} as your reward. It is the Lord {Kyri?} Christ (the Anointed One) you? are serving.
3.25: Whoever does wrong will be repaid for his wrong, and there is no favoritism.

4.1: Masters {kyrioi}, supply your slaves {doulois} with what is right and fair, since you know that you? also have a Master {Kyrion} in heaven.
4.2: Devote yourselves to prayer, being watchful and thankful,
4.3: as you pray also for us, that God {Theos} may open to us a door for the word, so that we may proclaim the mystery of Christ (the Anointed One), for which I am in chains.
4.4: Pray that I may declare it clearly, as I should.
4.5: Act wisely toward outsiders, redeeming (ransoming) the time.
4.6: Let your? speech always be gracious, seasoned with salt, so that you? may know how to answer everyone.
4.7: Tychicus will tell you? all the news about me. He is a beloved {agap?tos} brother, a faithful minister {diakonos}, and a fellow servant {syndoulos} in the Lord {Kyri?}.
4.8: I have sent him to you? for this very purpose, that you? may know about us, and that he may encourage your? hearts.
4.9: With him I am sending Onesimus (beneficial, profitable), our faithful and beloved {agap?t?} brother, who is one of you?. They will tell you? about everything here.
4.10: My fellow prisoner Aristarchus (of Macedonia) sends you? greetings, as does Mark the cousin of Barnabas (son of encouragement). You? have already received instructions about him: If he comes to you?, welcome him.
4.11: Jesus (YHWH saves), who is called Justus (righteous), also sends greetings. These are the only Jews among my fellow workers for the kingdom of God {Theou}, and they have been a comfort to me.
4.12: Epaphras, who is one of you? and a servant {doulos} of Christ (the Anointed One) Jesus (YHWH saves), sends you? greetings. He is always wrestling in prayer for you?, so that you? may stand mature (complete) and fully assured in the full will of God {Theou}.
4.13: For I testify about him that he goes to great pains for you? and for those at Laodicea and Hierapolis.
4.14: Luke (from Lucania), the beloved {agap?tos} physician, and Demas send you? greetings.
4.15: Greet the brothers (and sisters) in Laodicea, as well as Nympha and the church {ekkl?sian} that meets at her house.
4.16: After this letter {epistol?} has been read among you?, make sure that it is also read in the church {ekkl?sia} of the Laodiceans, and that you? in turn read the letter from Laodicea.
4.17: Tell Archippus: “See to it that you complete the ministry {diakonian} you have received in the Lord {Kyri?}.
4.18: This greeting is in my own hand—Paul (humble). Remember my chains. Grace be with you? [Amen.].
